perturbation.original_text: Within fifteen (15) calendar Days following receipt of each of Transporter's invoices and statements, Customer shall make payment to Transporter of all undisputed amounts by wire transfer, in accordance with the account information provided to Customer, unless another form of payment is agreed to in writing by the Parties. Should Customer fail to make payment in full of all undisputed amounts within such time period, interest shall accrue on the unpaid balance at the Default Interest Rate or the maximum legal rate, whichever is the lesser, from the date such payment is due until the same is paid.
perturbation.changed_text: Within fifteen (15) calendar Days following receipt of each of Transporter's invoices and statements, Customer shall make payment to Transporter of all undisputed amounts by wire transfer, in accordance with the account information provided to Customer, unless another form of payment is agreed to in writing by the Parties. Should Customer fail to make payment in full of all undisputed amounts within such time period, interest shall accrue on the unpaid balance at the Default Interest Rate or the maximum legal rate, whichever is the lesser, from the date that is thirty (30) calendar days after such payment is due until the same is paid.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering when interest begins to accrue on late payments. Originally, interest accrued from the date payment was due. The modified text specifies that interest accrues starting thirty (30) calendar days after the original payment due date. This contradicts the original implication that interest begins immediately upon missing the initial 15-day deadline, creating uncertainty about the exact period before interest charges apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Invoices and Statements. On or before the fifteenth (15th) calendar Day of each Month, Transporter shall provide to Customer an invoice and statement for the previous Month setting forth the calculation of the net amount due hereunder to Transporter for the Services provided hereunder during such previous Month. Payment of Statements. Within fifteen (15) calendar Days following receipt of each of Transporter's invoices and statements, Customer shall make payment to Transporter of all undisputed amounts by wire transfer, in accordance with the account information provided to Customer, unless another form of payment is agreed to in writing by the Parties. Should Customer fail to make payment in full of all undisputed amounts within such time period, interest shall accrue on the unpaid balance at the Default Interest Rate or the maximum legal rate, whichever is the lesser, from the date that is thirty (30) calendar days after such payment is due until the same is paid. If Customer fails to pay Transporter undisputed amounts due hereunder and such failure is not cured within ten (10) calendar Days' of written notice from Transporter to Customer, Transporter may suspend receipt and delivery of Customer Gas without prejudice to any other available remedies at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the point at which interest accrues on late payments, shifting it from the original due date to thirty days after. The contradicted text, which is actually the same text before the change, implicitly states that interest begins accruing immediately after the initial 15-day grace period. This creates a direct inconsistency and contradiction within the same section of the contract, specifically in the sentence 'Should Customer fail to make payment in full of all undisputed amounts within such time period, interest shall accrue on the unpaid balance at the Default Interest Rate or the maximum legal rate, whichever is the lesser, from the date that is thirty (30) calendar days after such payment is due until the same is paid.' in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text, compared to the original text. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different versions of the same section provide conflicting information regarding when interest accrues.

perturbation.original_text: Each invoice and statement shall be final as to both Parties unless questioned in writing within twenty four (24) Months following the date of such invoice or statement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each invoice and statement shall be final as to both Parties unless questioned in writing within twelve (12) Months following the date of such invoice or statement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the timeframe within which an invoice can be disputed. The original text allows twenty-four (24) months, while the modified text reduces this period to twelve (12) months. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding the valid period for disputing invoices, creating legal uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Audit Rights. Each Party or its designated representatives shall, upon reasonable notice to the other Party, have the right, no more frequently than two (2) times per twelve (12) consecutive calendar Months, at its own expense, at reasonable times and during normal business hours, to examine the books and records of such other Party to the extent necessary to verify the accuracy of any statement, charge, computation, or demand made under or pursuant to this Agreement. Each Party agrees to keep its records and books in accordance with generally accepted accounting practices in the oil and gas industry. Each invoice and statement shall be final as to both Parties unless questioned in writing within twelve (12) Months following the date of such invoice or statement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the timeframe for disputing invoices from 24 months to 12 months. The contradicted text also states that 'Each invoice and statement shall be final as to both Parties unless questioned in writing within twelve (12) Months following the date of such invoice or statement'. While at first glance it might seem as if they are aligned, the original text had a 24 month period, but the changed text modifies it to be 12 months. This clearly creates an in-text contradiction because previously invoices could be disputed within 24 months and now they can only be disputed within 12. This creates a direct conflict, and would lead to uncertainty and a legal issue within the document.
